title: Tricalysia sphaerocarpa
taxonomy: Tricalysia sphaerocarpa belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is one of the largest and most widespread families of flowering plants, with over 600 genera. The genus Tricalysia itself is part of the tribe Psychotrieae and is primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Tricalysia sphaerocarpa is an evergreen shrub or small tree native to tropical Africa. Its classification is further refined by its placement within the subtribe Psychotriinae.

It is believed to have properties that can help alleviate various health issues, although its effectiveness is not supported by scientific evidence. intro: Tricalysia sphaerocarpa, also known as African tricalysia, is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, commonly found in tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Bioactive constituents of Tricalysia sphaerocarpa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tricalysia sphaerocarpa is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is used to make various herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures.
